title: Extend `jit validate` and gate-checker integration
description:
Extend `jit validate` into a per-issue and whole-repo rule runner with `--explain`, and ensure gate-checker and MCP-schema parity.

## Background

`jit validate` is the on-demand validation entry point and the gate-checker target; today it takes no positional id. See `dev/active/f6a704d0-validation-engine-plan.md` (step 7) and decision record section 9.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] [hard] `jit validate` accepts a positional `[<id>]` (net-new surface) plus `--json`; it runs local and graph rules for one issue or the whole repo.
- [ ] [hard] `--explain` lists matched selectors to rule names to outcomes for an issue.
- [ ] [hard] Output reports rule name, severity, and message; the command exits non-zero when error findings exist.
- [ ] [hard] A gate invokes validation via the `JIT_ISSUE_ID` env var: `--checker-command 'jit validate "$JIT_ISSUE_ID" --json'` (no `{issue}` placeholder).
- [ ] [hard] The new positional and flags appear in `jit --schema`; an integration test asserts the MCP server generates a working tool.

## Notes

Anchors: `cli.rs` Validate subcommand 160-180; `main.rs` dispatch near 225/272; `schema.rs` `CommandSchema::generate`; `gate_execution.rs:52-59` sets `JIT_ISSUE_ID`. The server/web-UI surface is deferred (decision record 9.4).
